programmes.show_description: Lost at Sea: Witness a remote island linked to one of the world's most famous aviation mysteries and explore thousands of shipwrecks in the Bermuda Triangle. (S1, ep 9)
Phoenix Lights: Expert Chris Rutkowski, home to the largest UFO research archive, sets the scene and explains an unexplained event. (S1, ep 1)
The Top Ten Extraordinary Creatures: Ancient astronaut theorist Giorgio Tsoukalos takes a look back at some of the most mysterious yet incredible creatures ever investigated. (S16, ep 2)
Cameras capture footage of millions of strange creatures invading a church and some wonder if it's evidence of a grisly buried secret. (S4, ep 4)
Million Dollar Resolve: The Golds have the opportunity of a lifetime when they stumble upon pieces of art that could be worth millions. But will they be able to cash in? (S13, ep 1)
High Roller: Les hands out his biggest loan ever on a luxurious Rolls Royce. Will this latest gamble pay off or will another shady transaction leave Les's ego in tatters? (S13, ep 2)
The Drag Race: When Les puts the kibosh on Seth's outlandish plan to boost business, father and son head to the race track to decide who's boss. (S13, ep 3)
Join antiques expert and presenter David Dickinson as more collectors try to sell their items to dealers. Will they strike a deal or take a chance at auction instead? (S9, ep 48)
Chesterfield 1: David Dickinson travels the country, team in tow, hoping to snap up some good deals. But will the locals take the cash for their antiques or gamble at auction? (S10, ep 28)
Rarer Than Hen's Teeth: Rick and Chum show off their pearly whites when a seller brings in a pair of T-Rex teeth, while Corey goes to bat for a signed Mark Buehrle jersey. (S24, ep 18)
...Henry's Wreck-trospective. The Oakley Wreck-Trospective: Dive headfirst into the archives to celebrate the very best bits from the hit series Junk & Disorderly. (S1, ep 8)
Locktoberfest: Darrell and Brandon have high hopes as they return to the site of their biggest score. The others bid to take down the self-proclaimed "King of Montebello". (S7, ep 12)
My Little Brony: The action shifts to Oceanside, where Rene plans to make the others pay for crashing his home turf. Darrell and Brandon fight for a piece of film history. (S7, ep 13)
Claws for Cash: Scott shoots the moon on a bin that may cost him an arm and a leg while T-Money spies a prize that could send him straight to the top. (S2, ep 16)
Truth or Dare: T Money puts his honesty to the test with shocking revelations. (S2, ep 17)
Cameras capture footage of a strange, human-sized egg floating in Arctic waters. (S4, ep 9)
Thousands of UFO sightings and abductions are reported every year. This series examines these accounts and casts new light on historic cases. (S1, ep 3)
Lights Out: After the team unearths new and bizarre clues from the Mesa on Skinwalker Ranch, a complex laser experiment leads to a shocking encounter. (S6, ep 7)
The Pawnerator: Rick aims to get a rifle from the 1800s, a seller flexes a signed autobiography by Arnold Schwarzenegger and a ventriloquist doll makes Corey's head spin. (S24, ep 17)
Up The Ante In El Monte: Big lockers mean big money in El Monte where Brandi and Jarrod try to play the other buyers. But what happens if their scheme backfires? (S7, ep 14)
All Along The Swatchtower: Jarrod's antics push Brandi to her limits as the auctions move to Koreatown. With highend merchandise up for grabs, Dave intends to score big. (S7, ep 15)
...Henry's Wreck-trospective. The Oakley Wreck-Trospective: Dive headfirst into the archives to celebrate the very best bits from the hit series Junk & Disorderly. (S1, ep 9)
The Top Ten Hidden Alien Bases: Discover 10 locations around the world that just might be hiding alien bases, from a cavern beneath the ice in Antarctica to a Chinese lake. (S16, ep 3)
Kelowna: Expert Chris Rutkowski, home to the largest UFO research archive, sets the scene and explains an unexplained event. (S1, ep 2)
Claws for Cash: Scott shoots the moon on a bin that may cost him an arm and a leg while T-Money spies a prize that could send him straight to the top. (S2, ep 16)
Truth or Dare: T Money puts his honesty to the test with shocking revelations. (S2, ep 17)
Locktoberfest: Darrell and Brandon have high hopes as they return to the site of their biggest score. The others bid to take down the self-proclaimed "King of Montebello". (S7, ep 12)
My Little Brony: The action shifts to Oceanside, where Rene plans to make the others pay for crashing his home turf. Darrell and Brandon fight for a piece of film history. (S7, ep 13)
Chesterfield 2: David Dickinson travels the country, team in tow, hoping to snap up some good deals. But will the locals take the cash for their antiques or gamble at auction? (S10, ep 29)
The Top Ten Secret Projects: Secret agents, silenced UFO witnesses and hidden tech programs - could these mysterious projects prove contact with extraterrestrials? (S17, ep 3)
UFO Technology: Are UFOs spying on us? Do aliens have access to advanced technology? Tony Harris investigates compelling evidence of superior alien technology. (S1, ep 4)
...Mysteries of the Universe. Mysterious Earth: Earth remains a mystery to the billions of people on it. How did it come to exist? And how long will humans call it 'home'? (S1, ep 3)
Pushing Boundaries: When the team deploys a cutting-edge new technology on Skinwalker Ranch to study the mysterious Bubble, it leads to an unsettling revelation. (S6, ep 5)
Kelowna: Expert Chris Rutkowski, home to the largest UFO research archive, sets the scene and explains an unexplained event. (S1, ep 2)
The Top Ten Hidden Alien Bases: Discover 10 locations around the world that just might be hiding alien bases, from a cavern beneath the ice in Antarctica to a Chinese lake. (S16, ep 3)
When cameras capture footage of a strange, blob-like alien in the Amazon jungle, some experts suspect that the origins of this monster are stranger-than-fiction. (S4, ep 8)
Lights, Camera, Drama!: The Golds gear up to shoot a new commercial for the store which can only mean drama. Will everyone be happy with the final result? (S13, ep 4)
Karen's Big Idea: Karen brings in a Feng Shui expert in the hope of boosting sales and gaining a positive energy. But Ashley and Seth have some serious doubts. (S13, ep 5)
The Estate Sale: Ashley and Les head off to a huge estate sale, Seth takes a custom Corvette go-kart for a spin and Karen eyes up a piece of `70s movie memorabilia. (S13, ep 6)
Join antiques expert and presenter David Dickinson as more collectors try to sell their items to dealers. Will they strike a deal or take a chance at auction instead? (S9, ep 49)
Chesterfield 2: David Dickinson travels the country, team in tow, hoping to snap up some good deals. But will the locals take the cash for their antiques or gamble at auction? (S10, ep 29)
Detective Rick Tracy: One seller hopes to score with his Magic Johnson birthday invitation, while Chum takes a look at joker playing cards used as props in The Dark Knight. (S24, ep 19)
...Henry's Wreck-trospective. The Oakley Wreck-Trospective: Dive headfirst into the archives to celebrate the very best bits from the hit series Junk & Disorderly. (S1, ep 9)
Up The Ante In El Monte: Big lockers mean big money in El Monte where Brandi and Jarrod try to play the other buyers. But what happens if their scheme backfires? (S7, ep 14)
All Along The Swatchtower: Jarrod's antics push Brandi to her limits as the auctions move to Koreatown. With highend merchandise up for grabs, Dave intends to score big. (S7, ep 15)
Torching the Competition: Desert Dan returns, T-Money marches to the beat of his own drum and Brandon and Lori battle for a bin in order to protect their territory. (S2, ep 18)
Fuel to the Fire: Brandon and Lori look for a big win, the Alvarado brothers gamble and T-Money considers a fiery contraption. (S2, ep 19)
Diving into the unexplained using footage from surveillance cameras. (S4, ep 12)
Thousands of UFO sightings and abductions are reported every year. This series examines these accounts and casts new light on historic cases. (S1, ep 4)
Swarm Weather: A UAP appears above Skinwalker Walker Ranch during an experiment involving hundreds of illuminated drones. What happens next leaves the team in disbelief. (S6, ep 8)
Rarer Than Hen's Teeth: Rick and Chum show off their pearly whites when a seller brings in a pair of T-Rex teeth, while Corey goes to bat for a signed Mark Buehrle jersey. (S24, ep 18)
A San Marcos Mitzvah: The auction action moves to Darrell and Brandon's home turf, where Mary comes up with her own way to deal with inflated prices. (S7, ep 16)
North Hollywood Hustle: Even Dan and Laura fail to escape the fierce competition at locker auctions in North Hollywood. (S7, ep 17)
...Henry's Wreck-trospective. Sam Stratford Wreck-Trospective: Dive headfirst into the archives to celebrate the very best bits from the hit series Junk & Disorderly. (S1, ep 10)
The Top Ten Alien Codes: From glyphs in the Peruvian desert to spiral petroglyphs embedded with advanced maths, explore some of the world's most intriguing symbols and codes. (S16, ep 5)
Chicago O'hare Airport: Expert Chris Rutkowski, home to the largest UFO research archive, sets the scene and explains an unexplained event. (S1, ep 3)
Torching the Competition: Desert Dan returns, T-Money marches to the beat of his own drum and Brandon and Lori battle for a bin in order to protect their territory. (S2, ep 18)
Fuel to the Fire: Brandon and Lori look for a big win, the Alvarado brothers gamble and T-Money considers a fiery contraption. (S2, ep 19)

All Along The Swatchtower: Jarrod's antics push Brandi to her limits as the auctions move to Koreatown. With highend merchandise up for grabs, Dave intends to score big. (S7, ep 15)
Chesterfield 3: David Dickinson travels the country, team in tow, hoping to snap up some good deals. But will the locals take the cash for their antiques or gamble at auction? (S10, ep 30)
Full Throttle Semi Disaster: Outside a bar in Oregon, a woman is nearly crushed. A family on their way to a Father's Day picnic is cut off by an aggressive motorcycle gang. (S1, ep 13)
Houston, We Have A Problem: Drinks are thrown, fists fly and a car is rammed during an argument at an intersection. Plus, a lane change miscalculation turns into a nightmare. (S1, ep 14)
Lit Joyride: A motorcyclist in Colorado keeps his cool during a heated argument until the other driver touches his bike. A valet's joyride in Miami goes up in smoke. (S1, ep 15)
Strange Places: Join Dan Aykroyd as he takes a look at unexplained phenomena in the Lake Michigan Triangle and explores the most haunted place in America. (S1, ep 1)
Bizarre Deaths: From a 25-foot tidal wave of molasses engulfing Boston to a dancing plague claiming the lives of 100 people, Dan explores some of the strangest deaths on record. (S1, ep 2)
